narrative: OMX30 opens Monday in a technically mixed bull regime (+6.6% vs 200-MA) but faces headwinds from a sharply higher US 10Y yield (4.569%, +66bp), which compresses DCF multiples across tech and real estate. The weighted correlation signal is negative (-0.133%), and Asian indices sold off hard overnight (Nikkei -1.92%, Shanghai -2.06%), suggesting risk-off momentum despite the index's local strength. Brent crude +3.03% lifts energy but pressures industrials' input costs and consumer discretionary. VIX jumped 8.18%, flagging vol expansion that contradicts the weak ADX (0.8)—a strong mean-reversion risk if momentum stalls. Geopolitical escalation (Iran/US strikes) and memory chip sector weakness add tail risk.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a complicated Monday: bull regime intact (+6.6% vs 200-MA, RSI 52.75 neutral) but dominated by negative global flows and yield shock. US 10Y yields rocketed to 4.569% overnight, hammering duration-heavy sectors (Tech, Real Estate, Pharma) while Banks benefit from NIM expansion. Asian selloff (Nikkei -1.92%, Shanghai -2.06%) signals capitulation risk; watch 3085 (lower Bollinger band) as floor and 3123 (Friday close) as pivot. ADX at 0.8 is critically weak—reduce conviction by 30% per protocol. Oil +3.03% is a double-edged sword: Boliden rallies but Volvo/Atlas Copco margin-pressured. VIX spike (16.26, +8.18%) suggests vol premium; expect chop until US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET). Key watch: does OMX hold above 3085 or does yield shock trigger a retest toward 3050? Trade cautiously until macro clarity improves.
drivers: US yield spike (+66bp) compressing tech/real estate valuations
Asian selloff (Nikkei -1.92%, Shanghai -2.06%) signals risk-off sentiment despite bull regime
Oil +3.03% pressuring consumer spending and industrials input costs
VIX spike (+8.18%) to 16.26 contradicts weak technicals (ADX 0.8, RSI 52.75 neutral)
Geopolitical escalation (Iran/US strikes) creating macro uncertainty

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +66bp to 4.569% and likely SEK rate hike cycle implications expand NIM. Higher rates offset credit risk premium expansion.
Yield +66bp compresses DCF multiples. NASDAQ +0.28% overnight insufficient to offset duration headwind. Ericsson also weak on NASDAQ sentiment.
Yield spike (+66bp) directly pressures REITs' cost of capital and terminal value. Refinancing risk elevated.
Oil +3.03% lifts input costs (energy, transport). Brent 78.31 pressure margins. Weak ADX limits upside despite Volvo exposure to export strength.
Copper +0.47% and gold stable (-0.51%) provide direct revenue drivers for Boliden. SSAB benefits marginally from commodity stability.
Yield compression harms duration risk, but risk-off sentiment (VIX +8.18%) provides some defensive bid. Conflicting signals cancel out.
Oil +3.03% pressures household spending power. EUR/SEK -0.10% (weak SEK) adds sourcing cost headwind for H&M. Risk-off tone reduces discretionary demand.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.47% and gold stable provide direct revenue upside. Only commodity play with momentum in basket.
Banks benefit from yield spike. 10Y +66bp = NIM expansion. 4.569% SEK rate expectations support upside.
Industrials face dual headwind: oil input cost pressure (+3.03%) and weak ADX (30% confidence cut). Brent 78.31 margin drag outweighs export upside.
REITs most vulnerable to 66bp yield shock. Refinancing costs spike, terminal value compressed. 4.569% US yield pulls SEK rates higher.
Duration-heavy tech stock hit by yield compression. NASDAQ +0.28% insufficient buffer. Weak ADX (0.8) removes technical support.
Oil pressure on consumer spending + EUR/SEK weakness (-0.10%) = sourcing cost headwind. Double negative for consumer retail.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (consensus 238K) is key intraday catalyst. No major Swedish data today. Monitor ECB speakers for rate guidance given yield spike.
topRisk: Yield shock (10Y +66bp) + Asian selloff (Nikkei -1.92%, Shanghai -2.06%) + geopolitical escalation (Iran/US strikes) = perfect storm for risk-off cascade. ADX critically weak (0.8) at 30% confidence penalty means technicals offer zero support if macro deteriorates. VIX at 16.26 is elevated; any spike toward 20+ would trigger capitulation.
technicalSummary: MACD below signal, histogram -4.697 (bearish divergence brewing)
ADX 0.8 (CRITICALLY WEAK) — reduce all confidence by 30%
Stochastic K=28.6, D=37.3 neutral (no momentum directional bias)
Bollinger %B=0.40 (lower half, room to fall to 3085)
52W range: 89% — near highs but vulnerable to mean reversion

newsHeadlines.title: Jane Fraser’s ruthless remake of Citigroup
newsHeadlines.description: The bank has sold assets, cut jobs and made controversial new hires while cultivating a closer relationship with Trump. Is it enough to get back into the big time?
